preamble.summary: God speaks directly to Enoch, denouncing the Watchers for abandoning heaven and defiling themselves with human women. He explains that since the Watchers were spiritual and immortal beings, they had no need of wives or offspring. Their hybrid children — the giants — will produce evil spirits upon their death, spirits that will plague humanity until the final judgment.
preamble.remarkable: This chapter provides the Enochic origin story for demons: they are the disembodied spirits of the dead Nephilim, born from the illicit union of Watchers and human women. This demonology profoundly influenced Second Temple Judaism and the New Testament understanding of unclean spirits.
preamble.friction: The theological logic — spiritual beings had no biological need, therefore their taking of wives was pure transgression — raises questions about the nature of heavenly beings that the text does not fully resolve.
preamble.connections: Matthew 22:30 (in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels); Mark 5:1-13 (unclean spirits/demons); Ephesians 6:12 (spiritual forces of evil); 2 Peter 2:4 (God did not spare angels who sinned).

verses.text_reference: And He answered and said to me, and I heard His voice: 'Fear not, Enoch, thou righteous man and scribe of righteousness: approach hither and hear my voice.
verses.rendering: And he answered and said to me — I heard his voice: 'Do not fear, Enoch, righteous man and scribe of righteousness. Come near and hear my voice.
translator_notes: 'Fear not' — the standard divine assurance given to those overwhelmed by theophany (Genesis 15:1, Daniel 10:12, Luke 1:30, Revelation 1:17).

verses.text_reference: And go, say to the Watchers of heaven, who have sent thee to intercede for them: "You should intercede for men, and not men for you:
verses.rendering: Go and say to the Watchers of heaven who sent you to plead on their behalf: You should be interceding for humans, not humans for you.
translator_notes: This is the devastating core of God's rebuke: the Watchers' role was to intercede for humanity, but they have so degraded themselves that the roles are completely reversed. A human must intercede for angels — a cosmic humiliation.

verses.text_reference: Wherefore have ye left the high, holy, and eternal heaven, and lain with women, and defiled yourselves with the daughters of men and taken to yourselves wives, and done like the children of earth, and begotten giants (as your) sons?
verses.rendering: Why have you left the high, holy, and eternal heaven, and lain with women, and defiled yourselves with the daughters of humanity, and taken wives for yourselves, and acted like the children of earth, and fathered giants as your sons?
translator_notes: The rhetorical question format echoes prophetic judgment speeches (cf. Isaiah 1:5, Jeremiah 2:5). The Watchers' sin is framed as an abandonment of their proper place in the cosmic order.

verses.text_reference: And though ye were holy, spiritual, living the eternal life, you have defiled yourselves with the blood of women, and have begotten (children) with the blood of flesh, and, as the children of men, have lusted after flesh and blood as those also do who die and perish.
verses.rendering: Though you were holy, spiritual beings, living the eternal life, you have defiled yourselves with the blood of women and have fathered children through mortal blood. Like the children of humanity, you have lusted after flesh and blood — as those do who die and perish.
translator_notes: The contrast between the Watchers' original nature (holy, spiritual, eternal) and their chosen corruption (flesh, blood, mortality) underscores the willfulness of their fall. They traded the eternal for the temporal.

verses.text_reference: Therefore I gave them wives also that they might impregnate them, and beget children by them, that thus nothing might be wanting to them on earth.
verses.rendering: This is why I gave wives to humans — so that they might have children through them, so that nothing needful would be lacking to them on earth.
translator_notes: God explains that marriage and reproduction were designed for mortal humans who need offspring to continue their line. Immortal beings have no such need — their transgression was gratuitous.

verses.text_reference: But you were formerly spiritual, living the eternal life, and immortal for all generations of the world.
verses.rendering: But you were originally spiritual beings, living the eternal life, and immortal through all generations of the world.

verses.text_reference: And therefore I have not appointed wives for you; for as for the spiritual ones of the heaven, in heaven is their dwelling.
verses.rendering: Therefore I did not appoint wives for you, for spiritual beings of heaven have their dwelling in heaven.
translator_notes: Jesus' teaching that 'in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage but are like angels in heaven' (Matthew 22:30) likely draws on this exact tradition.

verses.text_reference: And now, the giants, who are produced from the spirits and flesh, shall be called evil spirits upon the earth, and on the earth shall be their dwelling.
verses.rendering: And now the giants, who were produced from spirit and flesh, will be called evil spirits upon the earth, and on the earth will be their dwelling.
translator_notes: This is the key verse for Enochic demonology: the spirits of the dead giants become the evil spirits (demons) that inhabit the earth. This tradition influenced Second Temple and New Testament understanding of unclean spirits.

key_terms.note: In Enochic theology, demons are the disembodied spirits of the Nephilim — hybrid offspring of Watchers and human women. This origin story for demons was widely influential.

verses.text_reference: Evil spirits have proceeded from their bodies; because they are born from men and from the holy Watchers is their beginning and primal origin; they shall be evil spirits on earth, and evil spirits shall they be called.
verses.rendering: Evil spirits have come forth from their bodies. Because they were born from humans yet have their origin in the holy Watchers, they will be evil spirits on the earth — evil spirits they will be called.
translator_notes: The dual origin — human and angelic — explains why these spirits belong to the earth (from the human side) yet possess supernatural power (from the angelic side). They are trapped between realms.

verses.text_reference: [As for the spirits of heaven, in heaven shall be their dwelling, but as for the spirits of the earth which were born upon the earth, on the earth shall be their dwelling.]
verses.rendering: The spirits of heaven have their dwelling in heaven, but the spirits of the earth — those born upon the earth — on the earth will be their dwelling.
translator_notes: The clear distinction between heavenly spirits and earthly spirits establishes a cosmological taxonomy. Each class of being has its proper place; transgression of these boundaries is the root of the current crisis.

verses.text_reference: And the spirits of the giants afflict, oppress, destroy, attack, do battle, and work destruction on the earth, and cause trouble: they take no food, but nevertheless hunger and thirst, and cause offences.
verses.rendering: The spirits of the giants afflict, oppress, destroy, attack, wage war, and cause destruction on the earth, bringing trouble. They take no food, yet they hunger and thirst, and they cause harm.
translator_notes: The description of demons as hungry yet unable to eat, destructive yet restless, creates a picture of beings in perpetual torment themselves — their aggression toward humanity is an expression of their own cursed existence.

verses.text_reference: And these spirits shall rise up against the children of men and against the women, because they have proceeded from them.
verses.rendering: And these spirits will rise up against the children of humanity and against women, because they originated from them.
translator_notes: The demons attack the very humanity from which they partially derive — a tragic cycle of violence rooted in the original transgression of the Watchers.
